sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 0.2g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, this really comes down to sugar control.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 0.2g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, this really comes down to sugar control.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 13.8g. fibre is where things start to fall off. for fruit, the real question is whether the sweetness stays under control.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 0.3g. protein quality is a real strength here. for a fruit, the cleanest wins are low sugar and decent fibre.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin c at 5% DV, vitamin a at 1% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side. hydrating whole-fruit package because water and whole-food structure make it easier to use well than juice or sweets. easy to use as a whole food because simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. brings a clean fruit identity because it still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side. hydrating whole-fruit package because water and whole-food structure make it easier to use well than juice or sweets. easy to use as a whole food because simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. brings a clean fruit identity because it still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides. protein contribution is tiny because that is normal for fruit, but it still limits nutritional breadth. sugar can climb fast because a lot of fruits look healthy but still lean sweet. satiety is not especially strong because fruit often needs help from the rest of the meal.

closing.summary: Apple does have some solid upside, especially hydrating whole-fruit package and easy to use as a whole food. but protein contribution is tiny and sugar can climb fast keep it from climbing higher. best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.

closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
